concerns.analysis: Page 20 in the JSON has an empty content array and an error message indicating an invalid JSON response from Gemini. This means the entire content of page 20 from the source PDF is missing in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 20 contains sections 46, 47, and 48 of the General Terms, and sections 1-3 of Services Terms.
concerns.output_quote: "content": [], "error": "[gemini-3.1-pro-preview] Invalid JSON: Empty response from Gemini (response.text was None)"

concerns.analysis: The image manifest indicates that all extracted images, including the primary NCDENR agency logo on the first page, have invalid base64 data ('valid': false). As instructed by the prompt, these corrupted images will result in broken image links when rendered in the browser. Because a meaningful organizational logo on the first page is broken, this constitutes a critical visual defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1, NCDENR official logo at the top of the document.
concerns.output_quote: Image manifest shows 'valid': false for the image with alt text 'Official letterhead for the North Carolina Department of Env', meaning it will not render.

concerns.analysis: On page 5 of the PDF, there is a table spanning from the previous page. The table has column headers ('Item', 'Description', 'Part Number', etc.) followed by data rows for item 2. In the extraction JSON for page 5, the table structure is corrupted: the first data row (for item 1) is placed at row_start: 0, the header row is incorrectly identified as a data row and placed at row_start: 1, and the second data row (for item 2) is placed at row_start: 2. This interleaves the headers into the middle
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table with headers 'Item', 'Description', 'Part Number'... at the top, followed by data rows.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a continuous numbered list of terms and conditions spanning multiple pages (items 1 through 48). On page 15, list items 13 through 17 have had their numbering entirely stripped in the JSON output (e.g., '13) Acceptance Criteria' became just 'Acceptance Criteria'). Furthermore, on page 16, numbered items 18 through 23 ('18) Deliverables', '19) Late Delivery', etc.) are extracted as plain text paragraphs rather than list elements, which breaks the list structure and na
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered list items: '13) Acceptance Criteria:', '14) Equal Employment Opportunity:', '18) Deliverables:', etc.
concerns.output_quote: Page 15 list items lack numbers: 'Acceptance Criteria: In the event...', 'Equal Employment Opportunity: Vendor shall...'. Page 16 items are paragraphs: {"type": "paragraph", "text": "**18) Deliverables:** ..."}

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (21): PR10689541: 3808 Upgrade to Cape Fear & Neuse River Digital ; https://buyer.ncgov.com/Buyer/render/1CB5X702R20R6[4/15/2011; https://buyer.ncgov.com/Buyer/render/1CB5X702R20R6[4/15/2011
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=21. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 3 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://eprocurement.nc.gov/; http://eprocurement.nc.gov/Vendor.html; http://www.ips.state.nc.us.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=3. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 2 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=2. Examples: p6: ed.daughtry@ncdenr.gov; p6: ed.daughtry@ncdenr.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 7 form(s) with 27 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=7, total_fields=27. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

The source document contains underlined text (e.g., page 6: 'Failure to execute/sign quote prior to submittal shall render quote invalid. Late quotes are not acceptable.') which is rendered in bold in the JSON. The prompt states: 'Underlined text in the source PDF that is NOT underlined in the output... that is genuine emphasis loss.' Since markdown doesn't support underline natively and the renderer doesn't receive underline tags, this is a loss of the specific type of emphasis (underline).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Failure to execute/sign quote prior to submittal shall render quote invalid. Late quotes are not acceptable.
concerns.output_quote: **Failure to execute/sign quote prior to submittal shall render quote invalid. Late quotes are not acceptable.**

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "pr10689541: 3808 upgrade to cape fear & neuse river digital "
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['http://www.doa.nc.gov/hub/', 'http://www.ips.state.nc.us/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 3 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=3. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
